summary.intent: Address Codex review feedback on PR #670 (ars-ui toggle group component) by fixing five accessibility/behavior bugs, then iterate through a second Codex review pass to resolve two additional findings.
summary.outcome: Both rounds of Codex review were addressed: first pass fixed aria-required scoping, button type attributes, aria-orientation scoping, reset-while-disabled behavior, and idle focus ordering; second pass fixed disabled group keyboard navigation semantics and the proptest Reset invariant. All changes committed, pushed, and CI gates passing.

repo: Toggle group uses SelectionMode::Single/Multiple to gate which ARIA attributes are emitted — aria-required and aria-orientation are only valid on role='radiogroup' (Single mode), not role='group' (Multiple mode)

Disabled state has two distinct layers: group-disabled blocks selection and sets aria-disabled on items, but non-item-disabled entries should still be keyboard focus targets (roving tabindex remains active)
Reset event must be allowed to change value even when the group is disabled — the disabled guard should only block selection events, not Reset
Idle focus seeding uses registered_items order, falling back to first/last enabled item
Items require explicit HtmlAttr::Type = 'button' to avoid default form submission behavior
cargo xtask spec validate is the command for validating spec markdown files
cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject is used for snapshot testing with rejection of unreferenced snapshots
PROPTEST_CASES=256 with --ignored flag runs the extended proptest suite for toggle group event sequences

code.finding: Focus/tabindex logic around disabled state — activation-disabled vs focus-disabled semantics needed separation here
ARIA attribute emission gated by SelectionMode — aria-required and aria-orientation conditionally emitted
Proptest invariant for Reset event needed updating to allow value change while group is disabled
Spec wording around keyboard navigation and disabled state required sync with implementation changes

workflow: Codex review is triggered by commenting '@codex review' on the PR and tracked via GitHub GraphQL API for unresolved threads filtered by author login matching 'codex'
The label 'snapshot-reviewed' must be re-applied after each remediation commit to re-enable the snapshot check gate
PR review thread replies and resolutions are done via gh api repos/.../pulls/comments/.../replies and GraphQL mutations in sequence

CI gate order: Snapshot Change Check → Formatting → Unit Tests → DOM/I18n browser → Integration → Clippy → Workspace check → Mutual exclusion → Error-variant coverage → Feature-flag/Coverage/Adapter/Release-verification (slowest group)
Two-minute polling intervals (sleep 120/180) were used to avoid hammering the GitHub API while waiting for Codex reactions

friction: Required multiple polling loops (sleep 120/180) with no event-driven notification for Codex review completion
nightly fmt check failed after applying patches, requiring a separate cargo +nightly fmt --all fix step before re-verifying
Five separate gh api calls were needed to reply to and resolve threads — no batch operation available
Codex opened a second round of review threads after the first remediation, requiring a full second fix/test/commit/push cycle
open_items: CI coverage, adapter, and release-verification gates were still running when the session transcript ended — final green status not confirmed
Codex second-pass review reaction (beyond 'eyes') not yet confirmed as approved/merged
PR #670 merge not yet completed in the transcript
